summary: Core plumbing is in place and matches the tech-spec for attachment state, ErrorLog whitelist, and Photo Picker integration. However, the single most important thing — actually sending attached images to the model — is not wired: ChatViewModel.send passes images = emptyList() and never clears _attachments after send. That alone breaks AC-13 end-to-end. Several secondary issues (LazyRow keying, scroll double-effect, unbounded onCleared scope, text state not saveable, dead code) should be addressed in the same round.

findings.issue: send() passes images = emptyList() and audioClips = emptyList() to helper.runInference, ignoring _attachments entirely. Attached images never reach the model, defeating the whole task 7 feature and blocking AC-9/AC-10/AC-13 end-to-end. Additionally, _attachments is never cleared after a successful send, so the thumbnails and memory stay pinned across turns.
findings.suggested_fix: Before calling runInference, snapshot the current attachments: val pending = _attachments.value; split into images = pending.filterIsInstance<Attachment.Image>().map { it.bitmap } and audio = pending.filterIsInstance<Attachment.Audio>().map { it.pcm }; pass those; then set _attachments.value = emptyList() as part of the same state transition that inserts the user/assistant messages (before runInference actually starts so UI reflects the consumed state immediately).

findings.issue: key = { index, _ -> index } cancels out any benefit of keys in LazyRow. When the user removes the first thumbnail, every remaining item's index shifts by one; LazyList treats this as 'content at key N changed', invalidating and recomposing every following Image. For Bitmap-backed thumbnails this is visible flicker on delete and unnecessary asImageBitmap() work. Task-7.md 'Details' note recommending index-as-key is incorrect.
findings.suggested_fix: Key by the attachment identity. Simplest stable option: key = { _, attachment -> System.identityHashCode(attachment) } — Attachment instances are created once in ChatViewModel and never swapped. Alternative: add a Long id field to Attachment and key on that. Drop the KDoc paragraph in ThumbnailStrip that justifies index-keying.

findings.issue: onCleared() spawns a fresh CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.IO) that it never cancels. The comment acknowledges 'GlobalScope is lint-discouraged' but this manual replacement has the same leak profile without the lint warning — if registry.cleanup stalls inside native code, the scope lingers until the process dies, holding a reference to registry. Also, nothing signals back that cleanup failed (no ErrorLog).
findings.suggested_fix: Use ProcessLifecycleOwner.get().lifecycleScope (process-scoped, cancelled on process death) or create the scope locally and cancel it in a finally: val scope = CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.IO); scope.launch { try { registry.cleanup(modelName) } catch (t: Throwable) { errorLog.e("inference-cleanup", "cleanup failed", t) } finally { scope.cancel() } }. Prefer the lifecycleScope path — cleaner and already process-bound.

findings.issue: MessageList has two LaunchedEffect blocks racing to scroll: one on messages.size (animated) and one on messages.lastOrNull()?.text?.length (non-animated). When a user sends a message, size and length both change in the same frame, so animateScrollToItem is immediately cancelled by scrollToItem, producing a visible jump instead of an animated glide. The comment says the second effect exists to avoid jitter during streaming — correct — but the two need gating so they never fire simultaneously.
findings.suggested_fix: Collapse into one LaunchedEffect(messages.size, messages.lastOrNull()?.text?.length) { val idx = (messages.size - 1).coerceAtLeast(0); if (messages.isEmpty()) return@LaunchedEffect; val lastIsStreaming = messages.last().streaming; if (lastIsStreaming) listState.scrollToItem(idx) else listState.animateScrollToItem(idx) }. Drives animated scroll only on message-boundary transitions; streaming deltas use non-animated follow.

findings.issue: MultimodalInputBar has 12 parameters, mixing input state (text, onTextChange), mode (isGenerating, hasAttachments, audioButtonEnabled), capability flags (supportImage, supportAudio), and six callbacks. This will get worse when task 8 (camera) and task 9 (audio) add more callbacks. Wide Composable signatures are a well-known recomposition hazard — every caller change forces recomposition of the bar and every child icon.
findings.suggested_fix: Group into two small holders: data class MultimodalInputState(val text: String, val hasAttachments: Boolean, val isGenerating: Boolean, val supportImage: Boolean, val supportAudio: Boolean, val audioButtonEnabled: Boolean) and class MultimodalInputCallbacks(val onTextChange, val onSend, val onStop, val onPickImages, val onOpenCamera, val onOpenAudioRecorder). Pass two args instead of twelve. Bonus: state class is @Stable-friendly if marked data class (auto-equals).

findings.issue: Nested viewModelScope.launch inside an already-running viewModelScope coroutine to log failed decodes. The outer coroutine is already on Dispatchers.Main.immediate or default; errorLog.e is a suspend function that internally switches to Dispatchers.IO. The nested launch saves nothing and makes the failure-logging fire-and-forget (no way to await it in tests, making snackbar+log assertions racy). The comment 'don't keep the caller pinned to log I/O dispatcher' is misleading — the caller is the VM coroutine, not a UI thread.
findings.suggested_fix: Await the logs inline: for (uri in failed) errorLog.e("attachment-decode", "decode failed: ${'$'}uri"). Sequential, deterministic for tests, no extra launch. If throughput is ever a concern, wrap the loop in a single launch — not one-launch-per-failed-uri which is what was probably intended anyway.

findings.issue: var text by remember { mutableStateOf("") } loses the draft on configuration change (rotation) and process-death restoration. Users typing a long prompt lose it if they rotate. The unused rememberMultimodalInputText() helper in MultimodalInputBar.kt appears to be a leftover attempt at this.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace remember with rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf("") }. Delete rememberMultimodalInputText() from MultimodalInputBar.kt (dead code).

findings.issue: InputBarHorizontalPadding is declared 'internal' with a comment 'ThumbnailStrip also uses it', but ThumbnailStrip inlines its own 12.dp value at line 51 and never imports this constant. Dead symbol + stale comment.
findings.suggested_fix: Either import and use InputBarHorizontalPadding from ThumbnailStrip.kt (preferred — single source of truth), or delete the constant and its comment.

findings.issue: Failed branch renders FailedContent outside any Scaffold/snackbarHost. Currently no snackbar is emitted while in Failed state so this is latent, but it's inconsistent with ReadyContent and will bite when Failed state later surfaces a retry action.
findings.suggested_fix: Either wrap FailedContent in Scaffold(snackbarHost = { SnackbarHost(snackbarHostState) }) or move the Scaffold out of ReadyContent and into ChatScreen so all three ui-states share it. Latter is cleaner.

findings.issue: Uses fully-qualified android.net.Uri in the parameter list (line 58) and androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Spacer at line 122, instead of importing the symbols. Inconsistent with the rest of the imports. A reviewer scanning the signature has to parse FQNs; also obscures dependency surface.
findings.suggested_fix: Add import android.net.Uri and import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Spacer at the top of the file; use the short names in the signature/body.

findings.issue: Attachment.Image uses 'class' not 'data class', so it has identity equals/hashCode. Fine in isolation, but the KDoc paragraph on Audio's custom equals/hashCode justifies them via 'recomposition keys and diff comparisons' — and yet ThumbnailStrip keys by index, so those overrides are not currently exercised. Keep the overrides (they are correct), but reconcile the KDoc once ThumbnailStrip keying is fixed (see major finding on ThumbnailStrip.kt:56): key-by-identity means Audio.equals is actually irrelevant, key-by-value would need it. Today the comment describes a world that does not exist yet.
findings.suggested_fix: Tighten the KDoc to cite the real consumer (tests, diffing, or future value-keyed usage) rather than 'recomposition keys'. No code change needed until the ThumbnailStrip keying issue is resolved.

findings.issue: send(text: String) checks text.isBlank() but later passes the un-trimmed text into both Message(MessageRole.USER, text) and helper.runInference(input = text, ...). Callers today trim before calling (ReadyContent line 187), so this is defensive-only, but if any other caller forwards raw text with leading/trailing whitespace, the user message bubble will show it and the model input will carry it.
findings.suggested_fix: Normalize inside send: val normalized = text.trim(); if (normalized.isEmpty() && _attachments.value.isEmpty()) return; then use normalized everywhere below. Remove the trim from ReadyContent.onSend — VM is the right place.
